CONTENT.Career: A youth product of Canet and Montpellier, Estève signed his first aspirant contract with Montpellier on 19 November 2019. He made his professional debut with Montpellier in a 3–2 Ligue 1 loss to Marseille on 8 August 2021. On 23 September 2021, he signed his first professional contract with the club.On 1 February 2024, Estève was loaned out to English side Burnley until the end of the season with an option to buy. On 21 May 2024, Burnley said the loan had been made permanent.
CONTENT.External links: Maxime Estève at SoccerwayMaxime Estève at FootballDatabase.euMHSC Foot ProfileMaxime Estève – French league stats at Ligue 1 – also available in FrenchFFF ProfileMaxime Estève – UEFA competition record (archive)
CONTENT.International career: Estève is a youth international for France, having played for the France U20s. His club, Burnley, denied him permission to compete in the 2024 Summer Olympics on home soil, and was replaced by Loïc Badé.

SUMMARY: Maxime Estève (born 26 May 2002) is a French professional footballer who plays as a centre-back for EFL Championship side Burnley.
